mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor market expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns via earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and credit stress, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production and retail sales creates a coordinated demand-supply deterioration signal that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint indicator of INDPRO and RSAFS both declining fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in housing starts predict negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because construction weakness foreshadows broader investment rollover and recession risk.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST changes do not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns during identified late-cycle periods (NBER expansion months after the midpoint).

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending that supports corporate revenue surprises and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS acceleration fails to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.5% that rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over two months signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.5% and UNRATE increases > 0.2pp over prior 2 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in > 45% of triggered instances during the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL combined with a falling UNRATE generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because a tight labor market intensifies expected Fed hawkishness, raising the equity risk premium.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and falling UNRATE does not produce negative 20-day returns with a t-statistic > 2.0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production index (INDPRO) surprises predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because production data resolves near-term growth uncertainty and triggers rapid factor reallocation into cyclical equities.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises fail to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.
